steps.title: Name the comparison
steps.content: The underlined phrase begins with much like, so it is making a comparison. It compares the colored filters placed over the mantises' eyes to the filters in familiar 3D movie glasses, helping readers picture the experimental setup.

steps.title: Reject other functions
steps.content: Choice B says the phrase emphasizes a difference between mantis research and other animal-vision research, but the phrase compares filter devices. Choice C treats the phrase as a history of a research tool. Choice D says it identifies a problem, though the text presents no methodological problem there.

steps.title: Choose the clarifying role
steps.content: The phrase functions as an analogy for understanding the praying mantis study. It explains the unfamiliar setup by linking it to a familiar visual device rather than adding a contrast, a tool history, or a research obstacle. The answer is A.
